devanagari: कुवलयापीडवधः भगवतो मल्लशाखायां प्रवेशः चाणूरेणसह संवादश्च । श्रीशुक उवाच । अथ कृष्णश्च रामश्च कृतशौचौ परन्तप । मल्लदुन्दुभिनिर्घोषं श्र...
english_devnagari: kuvalayāpīḍavadhaḥ bhagavato mallaśākhāyāṃ praveśaḥ cāṇūreṇasaha saṃvādaśca śrīśuka uvāca atha kṛṣṇaśca rāmaśca kṛtaśaucau parantapa malladundubhinirghoṣaṃ śrutvā draṣṭumupeyatuḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
The next morning, Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma who had taken their bath and performed purificatory rites (as if to expiate the sin of killing their maternal uncle who, despite warnings of breaking the sacred bow, did not release their parents but wanted to kill them, heard the sound of the kettledrums and the shouting and beating of arms by wrestlers, went to see the tournaments, Oh repressor of enemies!

devanagari: रङ्गद्वारं समासाद्य तस्मिन् नागमवस्थितम् । अपश्यत्कुवलयापीडं कृष्णोऽम्बष्ठप्रचोदितम् ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: raṅgadvāraṃ samāsādya tasmin nāgamavasthitam apaśyatkuvalayāpīḍaṃ kṛṣṇo
translation: Arriving at the entrance of the arena, Kṛṣṇa beheld the elephant Kuvalayāpīḍa stationed there under the direction of the controller of the elephant.

devanagari: बद्ध्वा परिकरं शौरिः समुह्य कुटिलालकान् । उवाच हस्तिपं वाचा मेघनादगभीरया ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: baddhvā parikaraṃ śauriḥ samuhya kuṭilālakān uvāca hastipaṃ vācā meghanādagabhīrayā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Preparing himself for action by tightening the cloth worn round the loins, and tying up tightly his curly locks of hair (with his upper garment) he spoke to the conductor of the elephant in a voice deep like thunder.

devanagari: अम्बष्ठाम्बष्ठ मार्गं नौ देह्यपक्रम मा चिरम् । नो चेत्सकुञ्जरं त्वाद्य नयामि यमसादनम् ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: ambaṣṭhāmbaṣṭha mārgaṃ nau dehyapakrama mā ciram no cetsakuñjaraṃ tvādya nayāmi yamasādanam
translation: “You conductor of the elephant I Make way for us. Hurry up. Clear out instantly. Else I shall send you along with your elephant to the region of Death.”

devanagari: एवं निर्भर्त्सितोऽम्बष्ठः कुपितः कोपितं गजम् । चोदयामास कृष्णाय कालान्तक यमोपमम् ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ nirbhartsito codayāmāsa kṛṣṇāya kālāntaka yamopamam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ kup class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ kup class 10 verb]

translation: Threatened thus, the driver of the elephant got enraged and goaded that already infuriated elephant looking like the god of Death at the end of the universe, to charge against Kṛṣṇa.

devanagari: करीन्द्रस्तमभिद्रुत्य करेण तरसाग्रहीत् । कराद् विगलितः सोऽमुं निहत्याङ्घ्रिष्वलीयत ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: karīndrastamabhidrutya kareṇa tarasāgrahīt karād vigalitaḥ so

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect middle third single], [imperfect passive third single]

translation: The big elephant vehemently dashed at Kṛṣṇa and seized him with his trunk. But slipping out of the coil of his trunk, Kṛṣṇa dealt him a blow and concealed himself betwixt his legs.

devanagari: सङ्क्रुद्धस्तमचक्षाणो घ्राणदृष्टिः स केशवम् । परामृशत् पुष्करेण स प्रसह्य विनिर्गतः ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: saṅkruddhastamacakṣāṇo ghrāṇadṛṣṭiḥ sa keśavam parāmṛśat puṣkareṇa sa prasahya vinirgataḥ
translation: The enraged animal could not find Kṛṣṇa. Guided by his sense of smell, he reached his trunk to Keśava (Kṛṣṇa) who forcibly disengaged himself and rushed out of his grip.

devanagari: पुच्छे प्रगृह्यातिबलं धनुषः पञ्चविंशतिम् । विचकर्ष यथा नागं सुपर्ण इव लीलया ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: pucche pragṛhyātibalaṃ dhanuṣaḥ pañcaviṃśatim vicakarṣa yathā nāgaṃ suparṇa iva līlayā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Catching hold of the tail of that extremely mighty elephant, Kṛṣṇa easily dragged him to a distance of twenty- five bows (or one hundred cubit) even as Garuḍa would do to a cobra.

devanagari: स पर्यावर्तमानेन सव्यदक्षिणतोऽच्युतः । बभ्राम भ्राम्यमाणेन गोवत्सेनेव बालकः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: sa paryāvartamānena savyadakṣiṇato babhrāma bhrāmyamāṇena govatseneva bālakaḥ

translation: With his firm grip on his tail, he turned him to the contrary direction when he tried to turn to the right or to the left to catch hold of Kṛṣṇa, just as a boy would do to a calf to revolve it either this way or that.

devanagari: ततोऽभिमखमभ्येत्य पाणिनाऽहत्य वारणम् । प्राद्रवन् पातयामास स्पृश्यमानः पदे पदे ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: tato prādravan pātayāmāsa spṛśyamānaḥ pade pade

translation: Then he confronted the elephant at the very front and struck him down with his hand. He ran before the elephant with that much speed that he thought Kṛṣṇa to be in his grasp at every step and he made him tumble down.

devanagari: स धावन् क्रीडया भूमौ पतित्वा सहसोत्थितः । तं मत्वा पतितं क्रुद्धो दन्ताभ्यां सोऽहनत् क्षितिम् ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: sa dhāvan krīḍayā bhūmau patitvā sahasotthitaḥ taṃ matvā patitaṃ kruddho dantābhyāṃ so

translation: While running sportively, Kṛṣṇa fell down on the ground, and promptly jumped aside. Thinking that Kṛṣṇa had fallen down (and lying there), the infuriated elephant struck the ground with his tusks (as Kṛṣṇa was not there).

devanagari: स्वविक्रमे प्रतिहते कुंजरेन्द्रोऽत्यमर्षितः । चोद्यमानो महामात्रैः कृष्णमभ्यद्रवद् रुषा ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: svavikrame pratihate kuṃjarendro codyamāno mahāmātraiḥ kṛṣṇamabhyadravad ruṣā
translation: Extremely enraged at the futility of his attack and being goaded on by the drivers, he dashed in rage towards Kṛṣṇa.

devanagari: तमापतन्तमासाद्य भगवान् मधुसूदनः । निगृह्य पाणिना हस्तं पातयामास भूतले ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: tamāpatantamāsādya bhagavān madhusūdanaḥ nigṛhya pāṇinā hastaṃ pātayāmāsa bhūtale
translation: The glorious Lord, the slayer of demon Madhu, approached the attacking elephant, caught hold of his trunk and felled him on the ground.

devanagari: पतितस्य पदाऽक्रम्य मृगेन्द्र इव लीलया । दन्तमुत्पाट्य तेनेभं हस्तिपांश्चाहनद्धरिः ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: patitasya padā dantamutpāṭya tenebhaṃ hastipāṃścāhanaddhariḥ

translation: Like a lion, Lord Kṛṣṇa pressed down the fallen elephant with his foot and extracting a tusk of that elephant killed him as well as his drivers.

devanagari: मृतकं द्विपमुत्सृज्य दन्तपाणिः समाविशत् । अंसन्यस्तविषाणोऽसृङ् मदबिन्दुभिरङ्कितः । विरूढस्वेदकणिका वदनाम्बुरुहो बभौ ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: mṛtakaṃ dvipamutsṛjya dantapāṇiḥ samāviśat aṃsanyastaviṣāṇo virūḍhasvedakaṇikā vadanāmburuho babhau

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Leaving the dead elephant there, Kṛṣṇa with one tusk in hand, entered the arena. With one tusk on his shoulder, with his body stained with drops of ichor and blood, Kṛṣṇa shone beautiful with his lotus-like face decked with pearl-like drops of perspiration’

devanagari: वृतौ गोपैः कतिपयैः बलदेवजनार्दनौ । रङ्गं विविशतू राजन् गजदन्तवरायुधौ ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: vṛtau gopaiḥ katipayaiḥ baladevajanārdanau raṅgaṃ viviśatū rājan gajadantavarāyudhau

[nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]

translation: Surrounded by several cowherds, Balarāma and Kṛṣṇa armed with an excellent weapon in the form of the tusk of the elephant, entered the amphitheatre.

devanagari: मल्लानामशनिर्नृणां नरवरः । स्त्रीणां स्मरो मूर्तिमान् । गोपानां स्वजनोऽसतां क्षितिभुजां । शास्ता स्वपित्रोः शिशुः । मृत्युर्भोजपतेर्वि...
english_devnagari: mallānāmaśanirnṛṇāṃ naravaraḥ strīṇāṃ smaro mūrtimān gopānāṃ svajano śāstā svapitroḥ śiśuḥ mṛtyurbhojapatervirāḍaviduṣāṃ tattvaṃ paraṃ yogināṃ vṛṣṇīnāṃ paradevateti vidito raṅgaṃ gataḥ sāgrajaḥ
translation: [1] When, accompanied with his elder brother, Kṛṣṇa entered the arena, he appeared like a thunderbolt to the athletes, as a superman to men, the god of Love (Cupid) incarnate to women, a kinsman to cowherds, a chastiser to wicked monarchs, a child to his parents, the veritable Death to Kaṃsa, the king of Bhojas, as a weakling or an inadequate competitor to the ignorant, the Highest Principle (Brahman) to the Yogins and the Supreme Deity to Vṛṣṇis.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā explains that Lord Kṛṣṇa was a combination of different rasas (sentiments) according to the view point of the person concerned. He quotes a verse enumerating the order of rasas. He manifested:
raudro'dbhutaśca śṛṅgāro hāsayaṃ vīro dayā tathā /
bhayānakaśca bībhatsaḥ śāntaḥ saprema-bhaktikaḥ //
VT. and Subodhinī also in a lengthy commentary expound how Lord Kṛṣṇa represented all the rasas. For example: the ordinary citizens were so much surprised to see the Supra-human exploits of Kṛṣṇa (e.g. killing a big elephant singlehandedly without the use of weapons by bis physical might) that it was a miracle to them (adbhuta)

devanagari: हतं कुवलयापीडं दृष्ट्वा तावपि दुर्जयौ । कंसो मनस्व्यपि तदा भृशमुद्विविजे नृप ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: hataṃ kuvalayāpīḍaṃ dṛṣṭvā tāvapi durjayau kaṃso manasvyapi tadā bhṛśamudvivije nṛpa

translation: Seeing that the elephant Kuvalayāpīḍa was killed and both of them were still invincible, Kaṃsa with all his intrepidity and self-possession, felt extremely terrified at that time, Oh King.

devanagari: तौ रेजतू रङ्गगतौ महाभुजौ । विचित्रवेषाभरणस्रगम्बरौ । यथा नटावुत्तमवेषधारिणौ । मनः क्षिपन्तौ प्रभया निरीक्षताम् ॥ १९ ॥

translation: The mighty-armed brothers with picturesque dress, strange ornaments, wreaths and wearing quaint garments, shone like two excellently attired brilliant actors on the stage and they captivated (and agitated the minds of the on-lookers with their brilliance.

devanagari: निरीक्ष्य तावुत्तमपूरुषौ जना । मञ्चस्थिता नागरराष्ट्रका नृप । प्रहर्षवेगोत्कलितेक्षणाननाः । पपुर्न तृप्ता नयनैस्तदाननम् ॥ २० ॥

translation: The occupants of the galleries, both from the town and the country gazed at those two super-men. With their eyes and countenances beaming with an outburst of extreme joy, they as if drank the countenances of those brothers with their eyes but did not feel satisfied, Oh King.

devanagari: पिबन्त इव चक्षुर्भ्यां लिहन्त इव जिह्वया । जिघ्रन्त इव नासाभ्यां श्लिष्यन्त इव बाहुभिः ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: pibanta iva cakṣurbhyāṃ lihanta iva jihvayā jighranta iva nāsābhyāṃ śliṣyanta iva bāhubhiḥ

translation: They were as if drinking them with their eyes, licking them with their tongues, smelling them with their nose and clasping them in their arms.

devanagari: ऊचुः परस्परं ते वै यथादृष्टं यथाश्रुतम् । तद् रूपगुणमाधुर्य प्रागल्भ्यस्मारिता इव ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: ūcuḥ parasparaṃ te vai yathādṛṣṭaṃ yathāśrutam tad rūpaguṇamādhurya prāgalbhyasmāritā iva

translation: They described to each other the details of what they actually saw (e.g. breaking of the sacrificial bow) and of what they heard (e.g. lifting of mount Govardhana) as if they were recollecting their personality (armed with the tusks of the elephant), excellences (such as bravery), sweetness (of smiling, manner of speaking etc.) and their extraordinary courage.

devanagari: एतौ भगवतः साक्षात् हरेर्नारायणस्य हि । अवतीर्णाविहांशेन वसुदेवस्य वेश्मनि ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: etau bhagavataḥ sākṣāt harernārāyaṇasya hi avatīrṇāvihāṃśena vasudevasya veśmani
translation: “These two brothers are the incarnations of the rays of the Almighty Lord Hari whose abode is cosmic waters, descended on the earth in the house of Vasudeva.

devanagari: एष वै किल देवक्यां जातो नीतश्च गोकुलम् । कालमेतं वसन् गूढो ववृधे नन्दवेश्मनि ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: eṣa vai kila devakyāṃ jāto nītaśca gokulam kālametaṃ vasan gūḍho vavṛdhe nandaveśmani

translation: It is reported that this Kṛṣṇa was born of Devakī and taken to Gokula where, without being known by anyone, he grew up in the house of Nanda till that time.

devanagari: पूतनानेन नीतान्तं चक्रवातश्च दानवः । अर्जुनौ गुह्यकः केशी धेनुकोऽन्ये च तद्विधाः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: pūtanānena nītāntaṃ cakravātaśca dānavaḥ arjunau guhyakaḥ keśī dhenuko
translation: By him Pūtanā, the demon in the form of a whirl-wind (Gakravāta), the two Arjuna trees, the Yakṣa (Śaṅkhacūḍa), Keśin, Dhenuka[2] and others of the same tribe were finished.
purport:
Footnote [2]:
It may be noted that Dhenuka was killed by Balarāma (vide 10.15.36) But people in Mathura heard a different report.

devanagari: गावः सपाला एतेन दावाग्नेः परिमोचिताः । कालियो दमितः सर्प इन्द्रश्च विमदः कृतः ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: gāvaḥ sapālā etena dāvāgneḥ parimocitāḥ kāliyo damitaḥ sarpa indraśca vimadaḥ kṛtaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

translation: Cows along with cowherds were rescued by him from the forest-conflagration, the serpent Kāliya was subdued and Indra was humbled down with his pride ridden off.

devanagari: सप्ताहमेकहस्तेन धृतोऽद्रिप्रवरोऽमुना । वर्षवाताशनिभ्यश्च परित्रातं च गोकुलम् ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: saptāhamekahastena dhṛto varṣavātāśanibhyaśca paritrātaṃ ca gokulam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: The big mountain (Govardhana) was held up by him on his palm for seven days and Gokula was protected from (driving) showers, (tempestuous) wiṇḍs and thunderbolts.

devanagari: गोप्योऽस्य नित्यमुदित हसितप्रेक्षणं मुखम् । पश्यन्त्यो विविधांस्तापान् तरन्ति स्माश्रमं मुदा ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: gopyo paśyantyo vividhāṃstāpān taranti smāśramaṃ mudā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ tṝ class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ tṝ class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ tṝ class 1 verb]

translation: Looking with delight at his ever-cheerful appearance beaming with smiles, the Gopīs easily[3] got over their sufferings of various sorts.
purport:
Footnote [3]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā alternately takes āśramam [āśrama] as qualifying mukham [mukha] and interprets: the slightly ‘fatigued countenance of Kṛṣṇa.’

devanagari: वदन्त्यनेन वंशोऽयं यदोः सुबहुविश्रुतः । श्रियं यशो महत्वं च लप्स्यते परिरक्षितः ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: vadantyanena vaṃśo śriyaṃ yaśo mahatvaṃ ca lapsyate parirakṣitaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ vad class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ vad class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ vad class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ vad class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ vad class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ vad class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ vad class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ vad class 1 verb]

translation: People say that being protected by him in every respect, the race of Yadu became widely celebrated and will attain affluence, glory and influence.

devanagari: अयं चास्याग्रजः श्रीमान् रामः कमललोचनः । प्रलम्बो निहतो येन वत्सको ये बकादयः ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: ayaṃ cāsyāgrajaḥ śrīmān rāmaḥ kamalalocanaḥ pralambo nihato yena vatsako ye bakādayaḥ
translation: This is his elder brother the splendid-looking Balarāma of lotus-eyes, by whom were killed Pralamba, Vatsaka (the demon who assumed the form of a calf) and Asuras like Baka and others[4]”
purport:
Footnote [4]:
As it usually happens in hearsay information, people of Mathura seem to have mixed up matters, for Vatsa and Baka were killed by Kṛṣṇa.

devanagari: जनेष्वेवं ब्रुवाणेषु तूर्येषु निनदत्सु च । कृष्णरामौ समाभाष्य चाणूरो वाक्यमब्रवीत् ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: janeṣvevaṃ bruvāṇeṣu tūryeṣu ninadatsu ca kṛṣṇarāmau samābhāṣya cāṇūro vākyamabravīt

translation: While people were talking thus (among themselves) and there was a flourish of trumpets, Cāṇūra addressed Kṛṣṇa and Rāma, and spoke as follows:

devanagari: हे नन्दसूनो हे राम भवन्तौ वीरसंमतौ । नियुद्धकुशलौ श्रुत्वा राज्ञाऽहूतौ दिदृक्षुणा ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: he nandasūno he rāma bhavantau vīrasaṃmatau niyuddhakuśalau śrutvā rājñā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ man class 4 verb], [vocative dual from √ man class 4 verb], [accusative dual from √ man class 4 verb], [nominative dual from √ man class 8 verb], [vocative dual from √ man class 8 verb], [accusative dual from √ man class 8 verb]

translation: “Oh Kṛṣṇa, the son of Nanda! Oh Balarāma! Hearing that both of you are respected as great heroes, expert in wrestling, you have been invited by the king who is eager to see you.

devanagari: प्रियं राज्ञः प्रकुर्वत्यः श्रेयो विन्दन्ति वै प्रजाः । मनसा कर्मणा वाचा विपरीत मतोऽन्यथा ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: priyaṃ rājñaḥ prakurvatyaḥ śreyo vindanti vai prajāḥ manasā karmaṇā vācā viparīta mato

translation: Subjects who abide by the wish of the king in thought, word and deeds, attain to prosperity. And if they behave to the contrary, they meet with the reverse of it.

devanagari: नित्यं प्रमुदिता गोपा वत्सपाला यथा स्फुटम् । वनेषु मल्लयुद्धेन क्रीडन्तश्चारयन्ति गाः ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: nityaṃ pramuditā gopā vatsapālā yathā sphuṭam vaneṣu mallayuddhena krīḍantaścārayanti gāḥ

[nominative plural from √ car ], [vocative plural from √ car ], [accusative plural from √ car ]

translation: It is quite well known that since the age as cowherdboys who tend calves, even grown up cowherds also take delight in sporting and wrestling in the jungle while pasturing the cows.

devanagari: तस्माद् राज्ञः प्रियं यूयं वयं च करवाम हे । भूतानि नः प्रसीदन्ति सर्वभूतमयो नृपः ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: tasmād rājñaḥ priyaṃ yūyaṃ vayaṃ ca karavāma he bhūtāni naḥ prasīdanti sarvabhūtamayo nṛpaḥ

[nominative plural from √ sad class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ sad class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ sad class 1 verb]

translation: Hence, let all of us do what is liked by the king. All beings will be propitious unto us thereby, as the king personifies all beings.”

devanagari: तन्निशम्याब्रवीत् कृष्णो देशकालोचितं वचः । नियुद्धमात्मनोऽभीष्टं मन्यमानोऽभिनन्द्य च ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: tanniśamyābravīt kṛṣṇo deśakālocitaṃ vacaḥ niyuddhamātmano

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ uc class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ uc class 4 verb]
[nominative single from √ man class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ man class 8 verb]

translation: Hearing that speech, Kṛṣṇa welcomed it. Considering that wrestling was desirable in his case[5]; he gave a reply appropriate to the place and occasion.
purport:
Footnote [5]:
Subodhinī explains that the investiture of the sacred thread of Kṛṣṇa had not taken place. As such, though he was a Kṣattriya, he was ineligible to use arms. Hence (for killing them) wrestling was appropriate from the point of time and place or traditional usage.

devanagari: प्रजा भोजपतेरस्य वयं चापि वनेचराः । करवाम प्रियं नित्यं तन्नः परमनुग्रहः ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: prajā bhojapaterasya vayaṃ cāpi vanecarāḥ karavāma priyaṃ nityaṃ tannaḥ paramanugrahaḥ
translation: “(Though we are not city-dwellers like you under Kaṃsa’s direct control) we too, wanderers in the forest as we are, are the subjects of the king of Bhojas we shall always do what is agreeable to him. It will be a great favour unto us.

devanagari: बाला वयं तुल्यबलैः क्रीडिष्यामो यथोचितम् । भवेन्नियुद्धं माधर्मः स्पृशेन्मल्ल सभासदः ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: bālā vayaṃ tulyabalaiḥ krīḍiṣyāmo yathocitam bhavenniyuddhaṃ mādharmaḥ spṛśenmalla sabhāsadaḥ
translation: We are after all boys. We shall play with those who are equal to us in strength. Let there be proper type of wrestling. There should not be the slightest transgression of justice, Oh judges of the wrestling tournaments.”

devanagari: चाणूर उवाच । न बालो न किशोरस्त्वं बलश्च बलिनां वरः । लीलयेभो हतो येन सहस्रद्विपसत्त्वभृत् ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: cāṇūra uvāca na bālo na kiśorastvaṃ balaśca balināṃ varaḥ līlayebho hato yena sahasradvipasattvabhṛt

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ han class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ han class 2 verb]

translation:
Cāṇūra replied:
“Neither You nor Balarāma is a child nor a teenager (from eleven to fifteen years of age). You, by whom an elephant possessing the strength of a thousand elephants was killed as if in a sport, are the foremost amongs [among?] the powerful people.

devanagari: तस्माद् भवद्‍भ्यां बलिभिः योद्धव्यं नानयोऽत्र वै । मयि विक्रम वार्ष्णेय बलेन सह मुष्टिकः ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: tasmād bhavad‍bhyāṃ balibhiḥ yoddhavyaṃ nānayo mayi vikrama vārṣṇeya balena saha muṣṭikaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ yudh class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ yudh class 4 verb]
[nominative single from √ yudh class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ yudh class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ yudh class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ yudh class 4 verb]
[present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation:
Hence, Oh Kṛṣṇa (A scion of the Vṛṣṇi clan) You will have to wrestle with powerful athletes. There is no injustice in this case. You measure Your strength with me and let Balarāma wrestle with Muṣṭika.”
[back to top]

devanagari: इति श्रीमद्‍भागवते महापुराणे पारमहंस्यां । संहितायां दशमस्कन्धे पूर्वार्धे कुवलयापीडवधो नाम त्रिचत्वारिंशोऽध्यायः ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: iti śrīmad‍bhāgavate mahāpurāṇe pāramahaṃsyāṃ saṃhitāyāṃ daśamaskandhe pūrvārdhe kuvalayāpīḍavadho nāma tricatvāriṃśo
